title: /etc/resolv.conf not being copied over
body:
### Issue Description

When creating a container, Podman (through podman-compose) doesn't copy over the host's /etc/resolv.conf. This blocks the container from using the host's DNS.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a container with podman-compose


### Describe the results you received

```
root@7f4a29ac2cc9:/var/www/html# cat /etc/resolv.conf
search dns.podman
nameserver 10.89.2.1
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
root@7f4a29ac2cc9:/var/www/html# cat /etc/resolv.conf
search aaa.bbb
nameserver      172.25.192.16
nameserver      172.25.192.20
nameserver      172.25.32.8
nameserver      172.25.32.136
```

### Privileged Or Rootless

podman-compose version: 1.0.7

comments.body:
This is expected, the ip is aardvark-dns to allow container name resolution and aardvark-dns will follow the host servers.

If you do not want aardvark-dns you must create the network with --disable-dns

Thanks! Creating a network with --disable-dns worked. The containers in the pod were still able to resolve each each other's names though, so I'm not sure what is meant by " aardvark-dns to allow container name resolution and aardvark-dns will follow the host servers" in this case though.

As a side note, instead of creating a network manually, you can also mount the host's /etc/resolv.conf in the compose file and have podman-compose create the network for you.
